Purpose: Vertical infographic showing hopper's plan-execute-validate-commit phase sequence, with the Anthropic profile's model assignments labeled at each phase

Style.Genre: Cyberpunk / Tech Noir with Infographic Elements
Style.Rendering: High-detail digital painting with clean graphic overlays, concept art quality, sharp readable type on holographic panels

Sources: Subtle magenta rim on connector arrows

ColorPalette.Treatment: Cool blue/cyan dominant for the pipeline frame, warm amber-tan accents reserved exclusively for the two Anthropic model badges so they read instantly as 'the spotlight'

PromptKeywords: warm amber spotlight on highlighted nodes
CRITICAL: every text label must be spelled exactly, no letter substitutions, no missing or doubled letters
exact required spellings: PLAN, EXECUTE, VALIDATE, COMMIT, Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Anthropic, next item, harness action, Model Invocation, Harness Action, HOPPER, ENGINEERING SEQUENCE

CompositionNotes.Framing: Pure infographic, no human figure, full 9:16 frame used by the pipeline
CompositionNotes.Layout: Four large stacked holographic node-panels running top-to-bottom, connected by glowing downward arrows. Title bar at top, phase nodes filling the middle, a small footnote bar at the bottom.
CompositionNotes.DepthOfField: Deep, every label crisp and readable
CompositionNotes.TypographyTreatment: Sans-serif, all-caps for phase names; mixed case for model badges; high contrast on panels

Situation.Name: Hopper Phase Sequence — Anthropic Profile
Situation.Setting: Abstract dark navy/black background suggesting a CRT or terminal field, with faint grid lines and a soft cyan vignette. No physical environment, no character.

Article.Name: Title bar
Article.Description: Slim panel at top reading 'HOPPER — ENGINEERING SEQUENCE' with a smaller subtitle 'Anthropic profile model assignments'
Article.Application: Top of frame, full width, cyan outline
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Sets the diagram as a hopper-specific pipeline view

Article.Name: Phase 1 node — PLAN
Article.Description: Large rounded rectangular holographic panel containing the word 'PLAN' in bold caps, with a small clipboard/outline icon. A warm amber badge attached to the right side reads 'Claude Opus 4.7'.
Article.Application: Top of the four stacked nodes; cyan panel outline with a warm amber model badge clearly highlighted
Article.SymbolicMeaning: The planning phase, executed by Opus 4.7

Article.Name: Phase 2 node — EXECUTE
Article.Description: Rounded rectangular holographic panel reading 'EXECUTE' in bold caps with a small gear or code-bracket icon. A warm amber badge reads 'Claude Sonnet 4.6'.
Article.Application: Second in the vertical stack; same cyan panel styling, distinct amber badge color identical in tone to the Opus badge so the eye groups them as 'Anthropic'

Article.Name: Phase 3 node — VALIDATE
Article.Description: Rounded rectangular holographic panel reading 'VALIDATE' in bold caps with a small checkmark-shield icon. A warm amber badge reads 'Claude Opus 4.7'.
Article.Application: Third in the stack; same cyan panel styling, amber badge matching the Plan node

Article.Name: Phase 4 node — COMMIT
Article.Description: Rounded rectangular holographic panel reading 'COMMIT' in bold caps with a small git-branch icon. No model badge — instead a slim subtle label 'harness action' in cool grey to distinguish it from the model-driven phases.
Article.Application: Bottom node of the stack; same cyan panel styling but visibly de-emphasized (slightly darker fill, no amber accent) so it's clear no model is invoked here
Article.SymbolicMeaning: The mechanical commit step, performed by the harness rather than a model

Article.Name: Connector arrows
Article.Description: Glowing downward-pointing arrows linking each phase to the next, with a faint magenta gradient pulse suggesting flow
Article.Application: Three arrows between the four nodes
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Sequential pipeline, one phase feeds the next

Article.Name: Loop-back hint
Article.Description: A thin dashed cyan arrow curving from the bottom (COMMIT) back up to the top (PLAN) along the right edge, with a small label 'next item'
Article.Application: Right edge, subtle, secondary to the main vertical flow
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Hopper moves on to the next queued item after commit

Article.Name: Legend strip
Article.Description: Slim bar near the bottom showing two color swatches: warm amber = 'model invocation' and cool grey = 'harness action'
Article.Application: Bottom of frame, below the COMMIT node and above any margin
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Reader can decode badge colors at a glance

KeyDetails: Four clearly stacked phase nodes — PLAN, EXECUTE, VALIDATE, COMMIT — taking up most of the vertical real estate
Amber Opus 4.7 badges visually bookend the pipeline (PLAN and VALIDATE), and the Sonnet 4.6 amber badge sits in the middle on EXECUTE
COMMIT is intentionally de-emphasized and unbadged to show no model runs there
Subtle dashed loop-back arrow on the right reinforces 'unattended worker, next item' rhythm
Every text label is sharp, high-contrast, and legible at thumbnail size
Situation.LightingFocus: Cool cyan glow on the pipeline panels overall; the three amber model badges receive an extra warm rim so they pop as the article's focal point

ArticleContext.Title: Seven Coding Agents, One Space Invaders Clone
